summary: deployment-key-auditor exfiltrates host and environment data to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: The package contains an install-time exfiltration payload wired into package.json (line 31) and a sibling scanner.js module that together harvest installer-side host identity and environment data and POST it to https://ddjidd564.github.io. Concretely: package.json's lifecycle script invokes Node with require('child_process'), require('os'), require('https'), reads process.env / process.cwd() / hostname, and POSTs to the hardcoded GitHub Pages domain. scanner.js (lines 4-9, 143, 161, 233-246, 298-323, 408) imports fs/os/https/child_process, calls execSync('whoami'), os.hostname(), enumerates filesystem paths via fs.existsSync, shells out via curl, and issues https.request/https.get POSTs to the same ddjidd564.github.io endpoint. The destination is an attacker-controlled GitHub Pages site (not a publisher domain matching the package), the data sent is installer host/identity/environment information, and the trigger is the install lifecycle plus require-time loading of scanner.js. The package's stated purpose ('deployment key auditor') does not require posting host identity to a third-party GitHub Pages site.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
